title: Subfield Separator and Profile Characters
text: Subfield Separator and Profile Characters Uniface uses part of the reserved area of each meta character set (that is, the first 32 characters plus the last character) to store characters with special meaning within Uniface, such as subfield separators and profile characters. Subfield separators are used in lists and subfields to separate list items and sub-fields. Profile characters are used as wildcards in fields to retrieve records that match the search profile. Context-dependent profile characters may be treated as profile characters, depending on the context. Literal characters corresponding to profile characters are part of the Font 0 character set. Table 1. Uniface Special Characters Character Font Decimal Meaning Subfield separator: GOLD ; 0 027 Subfield separator. Profile characters: GOLD * 0 016 0 through n characters. GOLD ? 0 017 Any single character. GOLD = 0 018 Equals. GOLD > 0 019 Greater than. GOLD < 0 020 Less than. GOLD ! 0 021 Logical NOT. GOLD & 0 022 Logical AND. GOLD | 0 023 Logical OR. Context-dependent 1profile characters: * 1 016 Asterisk, or 0 through n characters. ? 1 017 Question mark, or any single character. = 1 018 Equals sign, or equals. > 1 019 Greater than sign, or greater than. < 1 020 Less than sign, or less than. ! 1 021 Exclamation point, or logical NOT. & 1 022 Ampersand, or logical AND. | 1 023 Vertical bar, or logical OR. GOLD Character The GOLD character is an escape character that, by default, is displayed as a middle-dot Uniface special character. When a profile character, such as GOLD *, is stored in a field, the field will contain the GOLD character followed by an asterisk ( * ). In a Unifield, the profile character GOLD * is displayed as a highlighted asterisk. In an edit box, it is displayed as a middle-dot followed by a asterisk. The same applies for the middle-dot character itself. In a Unifield, the character is displayed as a middle-dot character, but in an edit box as two middle-dots; one for specifying the escape character and another for the middle-dot character itself. When the middle-dot character is saved into a field of a database, the character is stored as one middle-dot character. To type the middle-dot character, you must type it twice, otherwise the character will disappear when the field or Code Editor is refreshed. Uniface will process the twice-typed middle-dot as one character. The same applies for entering the middle-dot character in an edit box. On Windows, you can change the prefix character used when displaying profile characters in widgets using the PROFILE initialization setting. For more information, see Profile .
